name: Sendero Tajo de Quejigal (PR-A 56) y Sendero Tajos de la Sabia (PR-A 57) — circular desde Benalmádena
region_intro: Hiking around Benalmádena offers diverse terrain, from the rugged Sierra de Mijas mountains to extensive coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, including Monte Calamorro at 770 meters, providing panoramic views of the coastline and Andalusian countryside. Trails often traverse varied flora, including esparto grass, junipers, and pines, with some routes leading through Mediterranean scrubland. This landscape provides a range of hiking experiences for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Benalmádena The most popular hiking route is Sendero Tajo de Quejigal (PR-A 56) y Sendero Tajos de la Sabia (PR-A 57) — circular desde Benalmádena, a 5.4 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route combines two distinct trails, offering varied natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero de los muertos R-1 variante Calamorro — circular desde Arroyo de la Miel, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This trail leads towards Monte Calamorro, providing elevated views of the Malaga coastline. Local hikers also love the Puerto Viejo from Benalmádena — circular via the Sendero Tajo de Quejigal (PR-A 56) and the Sendero de los Leñadores (PR-A 55), a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through Mediterranean scrubland, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Benalmádena is defined by mountain foothills, coastal paths, and diverse Mediterranean vegetation. The network of hiking trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Benalmádena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Benalmádena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Benalmádena?
A: Benalmádena offers a wide variety of hiking trails, with over 150 routes available. These include approximately 77 easy trails, 69 moderate trails, and 11 more challenging routes, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Benalmádena?
A: Hiking in Benalmádena offers a dramatic contrast of landscapes. You can explore the rugged foothills of the Sierra de Mijas mountains, ascend Monte Calamorro for panoramic views, or enjoy scenic coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea. The trails feature diverse flora like esparto grass, junipers, and pines, often passing through Mediterranean scrubland.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Benalmádena?
A: Yes, Benalmádena has several excellent circular hiking routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304442171/sendero-tajo-de-quejigal-pr-a-56-y-sendero-tajos-de-la-sabia-pr-a-57-circular-desde-benalmadena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Tajo de Quejigal y Sendero Tajos de la Sabia&lt;/a&gt;, which combines two distinct trails. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304250392/sendero-de-los-muertos-r-1-variante-calamorro-circular-desde-arroyo-de-la-miel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los muertos R-1 variante Calamorro&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated views towards Monte Calamorro.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Benalmádena?
A: Benalmádena enjoys over 320 sunny days a year, making outdoor activities enjoyable almost year-round. While summer can be hot, the spring and autumn months offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. Even in winter, the mild climate allows for comfortable exploration of the trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Benalmádena?
A: Yes, Benalmádena offers several routes suitable for families. The region has many easy trails, and urban green spaces like Parque de la Paloma provide relaxed walking opportunities. For a moderate option, the recently refurbished Arroyo del Pantano Trail is a circular route connecting green spaces.

Q: Can I find trails with significant viewpoints in Benalmádena?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Benalmádena offer stunning viewpoints. The foothills of the Sierra de Mijas provide panoramic views of the coastline and Andalusian countryside. Ascending Monte Calamorro, either by hiking or cable car, offers extensive views of the Malaga coastline, the Guadalhorce Valley, and even the Sierra Nevada. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304471383/sendero-mina-de-la-trinidad-pr-a-58-circular-desde-benalmadena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Mina de la Trinidad&lt;/a&gt; leads to Cerro del Moro for breathtaking vistas over the Costa del Sol.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see along the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarapalos Spring&lt;/a&gt; is a natural monument worth visiting. For expansive views, consider reaching &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/633802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Calamorro — Summit View&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mijas&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seabird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; near the Guadalhorce River Mouth.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced hiking routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for more adventurous and experienced hikers, the Sierra de Mijas mountains offer challenging yet rewarding experiences with wilder terrain and significant elevation changes. The Cazadores Route, for example, is the longest in Benalmádena's network, crossing the highest parts of the mountain range through spectacular landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Benalmádena?
A: The hiking trails in Benalmádena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 460 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from mountain foothills to coastal paths, and the stunning panoramic views of the coastline and Andalusian countryside.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a glimpse into the region's history or geology?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304471383/sendero-mina-de-la-trinidad-pr-a-58-circular-desde-benalmadena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Mina de la Trinidad&lt;/a&gt; takes hikers past an old, disused ochre mine, allowing you to observe interesting geological formations before leading to Cerro del Moro for impressive views.

Q: Are there any trails accessible from Benalmádena Pueblo?
A: Yes, the Tajos de Sabia Route is an excellent option for connecting with nature near the urban center. This trail starts from Avenida Juan Luis Peralta in Benalmádena Pueblo and climbs to 700 meters altitude, offering a great way to explore the natural surroundings directly from the town.
tips: From this point we can enjoy the view of the entire Sierra de Benalmádena. We easily distinguish The Great Path of Málaga (GR 249), which runs through the entire province.

Section shared by several trails that pass through the Sierra de Benalmádena: the Tajos de Sabia Trail, the Mina de la Trinidad Trail and the GR 249, with the possibility of linking up with the Tajo de Quejigal Trail. Beautiful surroundings and views, with abundant vegetation, especially in spring.
